dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won a thin, mixed day because Claude drew the broadest product-specific discussion: one commenter called it "mind boggling" that Claude learned a niche language and produced compilable Apple Shortcuts [E2], and the Claude Code thread showed real everyday use even as users mocked the learning site's quiz [E3]. The offsets were real—OAuth friction in containerized setups [E4] and skepticism about Anthropic's security-research claims [E5]—but the overall Anthropic signal still showed more demonstrated utility than outright rejection.
winnerExplanation: Anthropic edged the day on breadth and balance. Hacker News had multiple direct Claude and Claude Code discussions touching practical capability, active usage, and workflow issues, rather than a single one-note reaction. The strongest positive evidence was a detailed report that Claude learned Cherri from scratch and generated working Shortcuts [E2], while the negatives were more about access friction and tempered expectations than a wholesale dismissal of the product [E4][E5].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude discussion mixed practical wins with real caveats: a commenter said Claude learned Cherri from scratch and generated fully compilable Shortcuts [E2], and the Claude Code thread showed serious daily use despite complaints that the tutorial quiz mis-ranked advanced users [E3]; against that, Coasts maintainers said Anthropic quickly invalidates injected OAuth tokens [E4], and another thread questioned how much Anthropic's 0-day-hunt write-up reflected genuine code analysis versus pattern matching [E5].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: The main Copilot story centered on complaints that GitHub Copilot inserted promotional "tips" into pull requests and on concern about GitHub's AI data-use terms, and the most consequential reply was a Copilot team admission that this was the wrong call and had been disabled [E1].

evidence.summary: In "Copilot edited an ad into my PR," top comments call the inserted PR message an ad, cite many similar cases, raise alarm about GitHub's updated AI data-use terms, and a Copilot team member replies that the feature was a wrong judgment call and has been disabled.
On the Cherri story, a commenter says Claude was able to learn the language from scratch and produce fully compilable Apple Shortcuts, describing the result as mind-boggling.

The Claude Code tutorial thread shows clear real-world usage—one commenter says they use Claude Code every day with plugins, skills, MCP servers, and subagent workflows—but the reaction is mostly aimed at a quiz that labels advanced users as beginners.

In the Coasts Show HN thread, the author says Anthropic rapidly invalidates host-injected OAuth tokens, making Claude Max plans impractical in their containerized setup and often forcing usage-key workarounds.

In "Vulnerability research is cooked," a commenter discusses Anthropic's recent 0-day-hunt write-up and argues the showcased results looked more like pattern matching and rescanning for known vulnerable patterns than deep code analysis, tempering the implied model capability.
